Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Tosco Corporation'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen J. Healy, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Richard Barron
Amee A. Mikacich, Esq., of BROWN EASSA & MCLEOD LLP (510-444-3131), appearing for defendant / opposing party Tosco Corporation

RULING:
PLAINTIFF RICHARD BARRON'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANT TOSCO CORPORATION'S PERSON MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AND CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel the depositions of defendant Tosco Corp.'s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for production of documents is denied. The cut-off date to hear discovery motions has passed, and plaintiff did not file a motion to re-open discovery, so the court cannot grant this motion. (Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568, 1585-88). Defendant's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff and his counsel to pay $1800 (6 hours at $300 per hour) due to plaintiff's failure to engage in any meaningful meet and confer effort prior to filing the motion. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
